title: Redesign weapon item sheet - modern, sleek, easy to use with Font Awesome 6 Pro, translucent look, using skill/talent sheets as reference. Keep 'rt-weapon-stats' banner for quick stat overview
Weapon Overhaul
Redesign armor item sheet - modern, sleek, translucent look matching weapon/talent sheet patterns
Migrate shared item panel primitives toward inline Tailwind
description: Move shared item presentation primitives away from stylesheet-defined layout and toward inline Tailwind utilities. Start with stat pills, stat bars, modifier rows, and shared panel partials that are reused across item sheets so later migrations inherit less generic CSS.
design: Update the shared Handlebars partials and generated preview HTML to express layout, spacing, borders, and typography with inline `tw-` utilities. Keep semantic classes only where they are still useful as hooks or where other selector-heavy surfaces still depend on them, and remove the specific shared CSS selectors that become dead after the migration.
acceptance:
- [x] Shared stat pill / stat bar markup uses inline Tailwind utilities
- [x] Modifier row markup uses inline Tailwind utilities
- [x] Item preview card stat pills match the new inline utility styling
- [x] Obsolete shared CSS selectors for migrated primitives are removed
- [x] CSS build and targeted checks pass
notes: Completed the first low-risk migration slice by inlining Tailwind utilities into the shared stat bar, modifier row, and description panel partials, and by updating item preview stat pills to match. Verified with local Prettier, stylelint, and a successful `npx gulp css`; repo-wide `npx tsc --noEmit` still fails due to unrelated pre-existing type errors.
close_reason: Migrated shared item stat primitives and removed dead shared CSS selectors.

title: Migrate shared item headers and quality tags toward inline Tailwind
description: Move the shared item header partials and reusable quality tag surfaces toward inline Tailwind utilities. This keeps the migration on high-reuse item primitives without jumping straight into the larger selector-heavy item sheets.
design: Inline Tailwind utilities in the shared item header partials, combat action header, quality tags partial, and ammo quality-tag markup. Keep semantic classes used as hooks or by remaining sheet-specific CSS, and remove only the shared quality-tag selectors that become obsolete once those templates carry their own utility classes.
acceptance:
- [x] Shared item header partials use inline Tailwind for layout and spacing
- [x] Shared quality tags partial and ammo quality tag lists use inline Tailwind
- [x] Obsolete shared quality tag selectors are removed from shared item CSS
- [x] CSS build and targeted formatting/style checks pass
notes: Completed the second low-risk migration slice by inlining Tailwind utilities into the shared item header partials, combat action header, reusable quality tags partial, and ammo quality-tag lists. Verified with local Prettier, stylelint, and a successful `npx gulp css`.
close_reason: Migrated shared item headers and quality tags to inline Tailwind and removed the dead shared quality-tag selectors.

title: Inline actor tab column layout primitives with Tailwind
description: Move the actor tab column wrappers and other simple layout primitives fully into template-level Tailwind classes. This keeps the migration on the low-risk actor surfaces that are already hybrid and trims redundant panel layout selectors without rewriting the panel internals themselves.
design: Update the biography, dynasty, status, skills, and talents actor tab wrappers to carry their own gap, width, and stretch behavior through `tw-` classes. Remove the dead or redundant wrapper selectors from the panel CSS files once those templates no longer depend on them.
acceptance:
- [x] Actor tab column wrappers carry their layout via inline Tailwind
- [x] Redundant wrapper selectors are removed from panel CSS
- [x] CSS build and targeted formatting/style checks pass
notes: Completed the actor wrapper-layout migration slice by moving biography, dynasty, status, skills, and talents column behavior into template-level Tailwind classes and deleting the now-redundant wrapper rules from panel CSS. Verified with local Prettier, stylelint, and a successful `npx gulp css`.
close_reason: Migrated actor tab wrapper layout primitives into inline Tailwind and removed the dead panel wrapper CSS.
